# Legacy GenDiscover HTML Contract

Use this contract as the source of truth for generated legacy HTML.

## Global requirements

- Generate a complete standalone HTML document.
- Use `<html lang="en">`, UTF-8 charset, and a responsive viewport meta tag.
- Inline all CSS and JavaScript.
- Do not load external assets.
- Use the inline SVG from `logo.svg` in both header and footer.
- Apply a dark report shell with accent highlights and card sections.

## Mandatory page shell

- `<body>` must use a dark background and a centered content container.
- The main report card must be rounded, shadowed, and bordered.
- The top header must show:
  - `data-testid="customer-name"`
  - customer label text
  - subtitle `Application modernization with Agentic AI powered by Capgemini GenSuite`
  - inline logo on the right
- The footer must show:
  - `Generated by GenDiscover`
  - generated timestamp
  - analysis ID
  - inline logo

## Visual tokens

- Use CSS variables for the palette.
- Required variables:
  - `--primary-dark`
  - `--report-background`
  - `--section-background`
  - `--accent`
  - `--accent-rgb`
  - `--report-text-font-primary`
  - `--report-text-font-secondary-rgb`
  - `--text-light-blue-rgb`
- Recommended default palette:
  - `--primary-dark: #081722`
  - `--report-background: #102737`
  - `--section-background: #163244`
  - `--accent: #1ec8d7`
  - `--accent-rgb: 30, 200, 215`
  - `--report-text-font-primary: #e9f7ff`
  - `--report-text-font-secondary-rgb: 184, 216, 232`
  - `--text-light-blue-rgb: 154, 198, 220`

## Data embedding

- If client-side rendering is used, embed normalized payload in:
  - `<script type="application/json" id="report-data">`
- The page script may read only from that embedded payload.
- Do not fetch local JSON files at runtime.

## Section behavior

- Sections should render as collapsible cards.
- Use open-by-default behavior.
- Each section wrapper must have a stable `data-testid` derived from the section slug.

## Content rendering

- Render Markdown-derived headings, paragraphs, bullet lists, code blocks, and simple tables.
- Preserve application IDs and scenario names exactly as provided by current run outputs.
- Escape HTML in user/content-derived text.
